expectations.evidence: Separate profiles created: memory/people/tanisha.md and memory/people/alex.md. Alex's knee documented as cumulative impact ('not an immediate problem, but several hours of stair climbing can add up'). Jordan's strict vegetarian diet (no meat/poultry/fish) clearly captured with implications. Language anxiety documented as main anxiety trigger.

Conventions section in CLAUDE.md (lines 66-82) explicitly establishes: USD currency (assume/confirm), Fahrenheit temps, miles, dietary specifics (Tanisha vegetarian, Alex omnivore), United 85,000 miles. Booking philosophy explicitly NOT hardcoded—marked 'NOT YET ESTABLISHED' with options presented for early Phase 2 discussion.

Conventions section (lines 77-82) explicitly addresses booking philosophy as TBD, presenting three options: Flexibility-first, Cost-optimized, Balanced. States 'This choice shapes every booking decision. Ask early.' Also listed as Open Question #2.

All required files created: CLAUDE.md, TASKS.md (with Active/Waiting/Up Next/Done sections), traveler profiles (tanisha.md, alex.md), memory/projects/japan-trip.md (referenced), memory/glossary.md, memory/itinerary.md skeleton, memory/sessions/session-log-apr01-a.md

Alex profile (memory/people/alex.md, lines 7-18) explicitly states: 'This is not a dealbreaker — it's a comfort consideration. Plan around it naturally (location selection, activity pacing) rather than treating Alex as having limited mobility.' Implications focus on natural planning strategies, not accessibility restrictions.

CLAUDE.md lines 162-190 explicitly outline Phase 2 (Route research) as next phase with clear subtasks: city research, weather, food scenes, crowd assessment, route decision. TASKS.md Active section lists 'Confirm trip dates with Alex' as next immediate task, followed by detailed Phase 2 subtasks.
